titleStmt.title: Letter from Col. F.A. Heygate Lambert to Sir Robert Chalmers, 26 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: F.A. Heygate Lambert

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Col. F.A. Heygate Lambert to Sir Robert Chalmers regarding the enclosed list of Irish prisoners to be released from Frongoch.
In 1916 Col. F.A. Heygate Lambert (1857-1929) was serving as the Commandant of Frongoch Internment Camp. Lambert served in the camp until the last of the prisoners were released in December 1916. In the Spring of 1917 he went to Ypres to command a battalion of the Labour Corps.
Sir Robert Chalmers was appointed Under-Secretary of Ireland in 1916, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the aftermath of the Easter Rising.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle,
Dublin, Sir, In confirmation of my telegram of to-day, I attach
herewith nominal rolls of Irish prisoners to be released
on Thursday the 27th inst., and who will travel by the
Holyhead and Kingstown route, leaving Holyhead by the
midnight boat on Thursday. I have the honour to be,
Sir,
Your obedient servant , F.A. Heygate Lambert. Colonel.
Commandant. Prisoners of War Camp,
Frongoch, nr. Bala. 26th July 1916
List of Irish Prisoners to be
RELEASED
at 8.0 p.m. Thursday. 27 July [Talbe not transcribed. See table/image.]
Camp No. W.O No Name Address.
955 1105 Brybe, Patk 2 Alexander Terr.
1715 1105 Byrne, Jas 39 Nicholas Street,
1809 836 D'Arcy, Jas Milltown.
369 625 Derham, Matthias. Roar Rock, Skerries.
1713 1582 Forde, JOhn 30 Up. George Street,
1556 603 Early, Patk J. Swords.
1482 1578 Kearney, Thos. Ballyboden.
1430 1211 Kelly Fergus. Coolgariff, Stillorgan.
1511 682 Horan, Matthew. 2 Anglesay Avenue, Blackrock.
1543 1536 McKee, Richard Finlas Bridge.
810 1480 McKenna, John C/O John Redmond, Forge, Milltown.
312 688 Noctor John 27 Dean Grange Terr. Blackrock.
1748 1560 O'Brian, Wm. 10. Annaville. Ranelagh.
1283 1440 O'Driscoll. E. Ashtown, Castleknock Colonel,
Commandant. Prisoneres of war camp,
Frongoch, nr. Pala.
27th July 1916. Certificate: Certified that I have examined the above named Irish prisoneres
and consider them in a good state of health. I am also of opinion
that internment in this camp has been in no way injurious to their
health. FRONGOCH.
27th July 1916. g.o.i/e.
